hub_subtitle: Inglês para o trabalho — do aperto de mão ao acordo fechado, em 7 aulas

lesson.menu_desc: Close the deal -- negotiate a better price, agree on the terms, and sign the contract. The final lesson of the program -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 7 -- Making a Deal
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ziláudio | Lesson 7 -- Making a Deal
lesson.grammar_point: would and could for negotiation and agreement

listenings.text: Hi Zilaudio, this is Diana. I am calling about our deal. I talked to my team, and we can raise our offer a little. Here are the terms: we would pay a higher price, but we need a longer contract, twelve months instead of six. And we would like the payment within two weeks, not one week. If you agree, I will send the contract this week, and you can sign it. Please think about it and call me back. I really want to work with you, and I think this is a good deal for both of us. Thank you, and talk soon.

Hi, this is Zilaudio, with some good news. We closed the deal with Diana this morning. We agreed on a good price, and the contract is for twelve months. The terms are clear: we deliver the soybeans, and they pay within two weeks. I signed the contract, and Diana signed it too, so everything is official. Diana is a good partner, and this is a strong deal for us and for the farm. I will send you a copy of the contract today. Thank you for all your help. Talk soon.
